aim.intro: The Request for Tender (RFT) is for the installation of a new Reverse Cycle Water-Cooled Heat Pump Chiller, as part of the procurement services required by Council to enhance and refurbish existing air handling units within their leisure centre facilities.
aim.body: This Tender Evaluation and Probity Plan (TEPP) is the planning and control document in conducting the evaluation of tenders received in response to the RFT. The TEPP sets out: â€¢ processes and principles to be followed when evaluating Tenders; â€¢ individual's responsibilities, including ensuring all work complies with WorkSafe NSW regulations and Council WHS requirements; â€¢ an agreed schedule for works that considers centre's operational hours and minimizes disruption during noisy operations or interruptions to existing services. The TEPP also outlines the need for proper recycling of redundant equipment, coordination with leisure center management regarding accessibility issues due to hoisting activities, compliance with electrical codes including switchboard requirements as per AS 61439 and associated works such as removal/re-instatement of plantroom external wall louvres. It emphasizes the importance of making good all disturbed work surfaces post completion, ensuring a tidy site condition upon project conclusion.

description_of_requirement: The Blacktown Leisure Centre Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation AHU Refurbishments involve the installation of a new Reverse Cycle Water-Cooled Heat Pump Chiller, as part of an extensive refurbishment project. This includes updating and upgrading building management systems to integrate with the newly installed chillers.
The work is scheduled around the leisure centre's operating hours to minimize disruption during peak times.

General:
â€¢ All works must comply with Australian Standards, particularly AS1677.2 and AS1210 for chiller systems.
â€¢ Licensed Personnel Only signage is required in areas where live parts are accessible to ensure safety during switch operations.
â€¢ Cables not enclosed shall be neatly arranged and supported as per the drawings provided, with a maximum of two cables terminating at any one terminal point for power connections.
â€¢ The Contractor must have an Electrical Contractors license in NSW to carry out electrical work on-site.
â€¢ Comply with relevant AS/NZS standards and NSW WHS legislation
â€¢ Hold all required licences and insurances before commencing works
â€¢ Provide detailed program and milestones; notify of delays immediately
â€¢ Attend mandatory site visit and verify dimensions/conditions
technical_requirements:
Switchboards Compliance:
â€¢ Provide a plinth with SS Tray under the feet of each closed system, drained to waste.
â€¢ Install corrosion coupons for testing every three months in compliance with AS/NZS standards and local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
â€¢ Ensure all equipment supplied and work done adheres strictly to this specification along with S.A.A. Wiring Rules, latest issue or amendment used where applicable.
â€¢ Verify switchboard compliance through a type test certificate from an accredited testing laboratory as per AS 61439 standards and local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
â€¢ Label all sections of the SCA clearly with 'Authorised Personnel Only' for safety, except where specified otherwise in drawings or by other contexts requiring different labeling to comply with NSW/Australian best practices.
â€¢ Arrange connections neatly and securely as per AS 3000 standards, ensuring no more than two cables terminate at any one terminal point unless specifically stated for Compressor and Condenser motors which may be directly terminated on respective Contactors/over-loads in compliance with local regulations.
â€¢ Install all wiring neatly arranged as per Electrical Design Guide requirements, using V75 thermoplastic insulated copper stranded cables of Australian manufacture where applicable and avoiding statutory fire rating unless specified for certain installations to adhere strictly to NSW/Australian standards.
â€¢ Ensure all conduits and ducts access is provided by removable aluminium gland plates, with front switchboard doors fully dust sealed as per best practices in the industry.
â€¢ Install wiring penetrating a fire wall using special intumescent fittings to comply with NSW/Australian standards for safety and integrity of building services installations.
â€¢ Conduct comprehensive testing including visual inspections, water-test drains checks, power cabling terminations megger tests, control cables electrical continuity verifications before requesting Council attendance to ensure full compliance with the Electrical Design Guide as per NSW/Australian standards.
â€¢ Provide fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection prior to any testing taking place in accordance with best practices and local council requirements, ensuring a clear record of all tests carried out is maintained.

Electrical Installation Requirements:
â€¢ All equipment supplied and work done shall comply with the specification, S.A.A Wiring Rules (AS/NZS 4829), any other relevant publications covering design, manufacture or installation of electrical equipment.
â€¢ Use only a licensed electrician for all on-site tasks to ensure safety and adherence to regulations.
â€¢ Ensure that the switchboard complies with AS/NZS 61439 standards. The supplier must provide proof through type test certificates from an accredited testing laboratory.
â€¢ Label all sections of the SCA clearly as indicated on drawings, including 'Authorised Personnel Only' for switching sub-systems and circuitry to prevent unauthorized access.
â€¢ Provide LED pilot light indications with clear run/fault labels for main components. Individual control fault lights must also be provided.
â€¢ All cables not enclosed shall be neatly arranged, loosely tied where necessary, supported neatly and terminated at approved terminal blocks without exceeding two terminations per block.
â€¢ Direct power cabling to Compressor and Condenser motors may terminate on respective Contactors/over-loads as specified in the drawings.
â€¢ Install all wiring penetrating a fire wall using special intumescent fittings rated for that duty, following AS3000 standards where applicable.
â€¢ Conduct testing and commissioning before Council inspections. Submit detailed Test Log sheets on A4 size prior to any tests taking place.

Chiller Product Data Provision:
â€¢ Provide manufacturer's rated performance data for the Chiller offered.
â€¢ Supply full dimensional details in certified drawings, including dimensions and finishes of all components to be installed on-site.
â€¢ Include weights with loading diagrams detailing each component's weight capacity and distribution points within a chilled water system.
â€¢ Detail facilities required for Chiller operation such as power supply specifications (voltage, frequency), control panel interfaces, alarm systems, monitoring equipment, safety shut-off mechanisms, etc., ensuring compliance with AS1677.2 standards and local legislation like SafeWork NSW.
â€¢ List accessories provided by the manufacturer such as spare parts for compressors (scroll type), evaporator heat exchangers made from Type 316L stainless steel, condensate sumps with associated fittings, expansion devices and isolation valves. Include details on installation instructions.
â€¢ Provide electrical requirements including but not limited to power source specifications (AC/DC), voltage ratings, current draws for compressors and other chiller components, wiring diagrams showing all connections with proper color coding as per AS1210 standards. Ensure compatibility with existing BMCS.
â€¢ Ensure conformance evidence is provided in the form of certificates or test reports demonstrating compliance with relevant Australian Standards (AS/NZS) for chiller systems, particularly focusing on safety and performance aspects as per AS1677.2 standards.

Accessories for Chiller Connection:
â€¢ Provide manufacturer's rated performance data, full dimensional details with certified drawings.
â€¢ Include weights and loading diagrams in the tender submission.
â€¢ Detail facilities and services required to connect new chiller into CHW piping reticulation system.
â€¢ List accessories provided with or as part of pipework modifications for connection compatibility.
â€¢ Specify electrical requirements, including wiring diagrams and compliance evidence (AS1677.2 & AS1210).
â€¢ Ensure factory testing at full thermal load is completed before delivery to site.
â€¢ Engage local distributor for maintenance during Defects Liability Period without additional cost.
â€¢ Upgrade Building Management Control System (BMCS) and integrate with BMS, including necessary programming details.
â€¢ Allow progressive fine-tuning of control logic up to the end of the Defects Liability Period at no extra charge.
â€¢ Install compressors as full hermetic scroll type complete with all specified components for vibration isolation (minimum 95%).
â€¢ Ensure installation and associated works are carried out in good engineering practice, adhering to relevant codes and standards.

Coil Design and Performance Standards:
â€¢ Design coils with condensate trays to collect all occurring inside the unit.
â€¢ Use globe type valves in AS/NZS standards, key operated c/w sealed caps for DN 50 and above. Ensure no distortion from heat on smaller pipes (less than 25 mm).
â€¢ Install drive slide cross joints airtight at corners using metal tongues or similar materials.
â€¢ Ensure ductwork is constructed in accordance with AS 4254, made of prime quality lock forming galvanised steel to G3 grade and Z275 coating as per AS 1397 standards for fire resistance (A2-S rating).
â€¢ Provide condensate sumps common to the cool side of heat recovery coils.
â€¢ Install finned EC fans in each AHU at a capacity of 2,800 l/s and replace centrifugal exhaust fans with corrosion-resistant supports. Ensure ade01 - Mechanical Services â€“ Air & Water Schematic includes existing equipment schedule SKM-01 for AHU -1&3.
â€¢ Design condenser heat exchangers as plate type fabricated from Type 316L stainless steel, copper brazed with overall insulation of 19mm closed cell foam. Condensers to be tested and stamped in accordance with European PED 97/23/EC Code.
â€¢ Install evaporator and condenser circulating pumps speed controlled by a variable speed drive control, primarily for performance improvement at IP55 enclosures.
â€¢ Ensure all mechanical works comply fully with BCC WHS requirements. Allow cranage lifts to suit the staged nature of carrying out work as per Council's Cranage Strategy and ensure hoisting facilities are suitable before ordering equipment.
invoicing: Invoices must be itemised and reference purchase order and contract number
Standard 30 day payment terms from receipt of valid tax invoice
Milestone/progress claims to align with agreed program and deliverables
Variations require prior written approval by Council
reporting: Weekly progress report with status, risks, and updated program
Monthly summary including KPI performance and incidents
Final report pack including commissioning results and certificates
Provide as-built drawings, O&M manuals and warranties

performance_monitoring.note: The tender document outlines the comprehensive requirements for a refurbishment project at Blacktown Leisure Centre's Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation AHU systems, specifically focusing on BLCS-001 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS. The contractor is responsible for ensuring all wiring penetrates the fire wall with specialized intumescent fittings and must submit detailed test logs prior to testing.
items: Quality of workmanship and defect rate
Compliance with WHS and environmental obligations

desired_outcomes: Strong WHS culture with zero harm
Value for money over the whole-of-life
purpose_rft: Set out the requirements, terms and evaluation method for the procurement
Identify either a single supplier or a panel arrangement as permitted by legislation
Achieve best value consistent with Council policies and probity

risks.action: FAT; point-to-point I/O test; SAT with witness
Verify duty; commissioning with TAB report

evaluation_schedule.task: Review Testing & Commissioning plan and witness tests
Review maintenance schedules and O&M handover
Review BMCS HLI/point list and integration test records

tender_evaluation.methodology_text: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced here, will evaluate all tenders.

required_criteria_table.rationale: Given the high commissioning complexity and safety critical nature of this project with a warranty period extending up to one year, it's crucial that cost does not exceed our budget constraints. Therefore, we allocate more weight here

With 8 EC fans in operation requiring specialized knowledge for integration into the existing system and considering past performance on similar projects is essential; hence a significant portion of the evaluation will be based on experience to ensure reliability and efficiency

The capability score reflects our need for robust, reliable equipment that can handle complex tasks with minimal downtime. A higher weight ensures we select suppliers who are not only experienced but also technically capable of delivering the required performance standards

Adegya's financial stability and management skills will be crucial for handling potential cost overruns or unforeseen expenses, hence a moderate weight is assigned to this criterion. It ensures that we can rely on their ability to manage the project effectively

Given our commitment to maintaining high-quality standards and minimizing disru0125 7e9rsees, quality management holds a significant weight in selection as it directly impacts system reliability. A higher score here indicates better adherence to industry best practices
Workplace Health & Safety is important but given the lower risk factors associated with this project and its non-critical nature compared to others, we assign a slightly lesser weight

evaluation_methodology.overview: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the evaluation methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced in this document, will evaluate all tenders. Compliance is generally taken to mean submission of the Tender by the closing date, in accordance with all lodgement instructions and provision of all information requested in the RFT (including any late tender provisions). Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted; depending on the nature, extent and implications of partial or non-compliance, a Tender may be disqualified from further evaluation.

compliance_criteria_table.title: 6.1.1 Compliance Criteria
notes: Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted. Non-compliant tenders may be excluded from further evaluation.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Exceptional
non_price_rating_scale.description: Fully compliant; no risks or weaknesses.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Superior
non_price_rating_scale.description: Sound achievement; minor risks/weaknesses may be acceptable.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Good
non_price_rating_scale.description: Reasonable achievement; errors/risks can be corrected with minimal effort.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Adequate
non_price_rating_scale.description: Minimal achievement; issues possible to correct/overcome.

returnable_scoring.note: See evaluation workbook and schedules for detailed rubrics.

pricing.pricing_model_text:
Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score (equal to the price weighting). State Government method:
Ps = 200 â€“ (Pc / Pav Ã— 100)
Pn = Ps / 200
Pw = Pn Ã— [Price Weight]
Pc = Total Net Cost, Pav = Average of Total Net Costs.

local_preference_policy.blacktown_lga_discount: 5% discount capped at $50,000 for suppliers located in Blacktown City LGA
local_preference_policy.western_sydney_discount: 2.5% discount capped at $25,000 for suppliers located in Western Sydney councils
definitions: Blacktown City Local supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within Blacktown City LGA.
Western Sydney Council supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within a Western Sydney Council LGA.
local_preference_policy.application_note: For any suppliers eligible for local preference, provide the confirming address in the third tab of the tender evaluation spreadsheet.

value_for_money: Assessed on qualitative outcomes + price: quality and fitness for purpose vs whole-of-life costs vs risk.
Consider whole-of-life costs incl. maintenance and warranty obligations
Energy efficiency and control strategy for chillers/AHUs
â€˜Value for Moneyâ€™ is assessed on the combined outcomes of qualitative criteria and price.
Consider quality of proposed services against whole-of-life costs and risk (capacity to deliver on-time and on-budget).
pricing.interviews_and_presentation_criteria: Scores may be revised following interviews/presentations of shortlisted tenderers.

model.narrative: Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score equal to the Price criterion weighting. In accordance with State Government scoring, the Total Net Cost for tender evaluation is normalised and weighted for comparison as follows.
variables: Pc = Total Net Cost calculated for tender evaluation
Pav = Average of all Total Net Costs
notes: The total score each Tenderer receives provides a numeric basis for comparison.
Recommendation on the preferred Tender will be based on scoring comparisons.
The basis of any increase will be calculated for the full term of the contract, including optional extensions.

tepp.json.contract_negotiations: A period of negotiation with the successful Tenderer may arise following completion of the Tender phase and will commence subject to approval by the Director. This period will result in the executed copies of the contractual documentation being completed and will necessitate a combination of meetings comprising formal written obligations to be resolved. The role of the negotiators is to ensure that the negotiation approach is appropriate to the nature of the project, is open and fair, meets the needs of the Tenderer and can be accommodated within the Tendererâ€™s resources. Negotiations must not result in material changes to the requirements of the Tender. The outcome of the negotiations will be reflected in the final contract and all negotiation discussions and outcomes will be documented.
tepp.json.debriefing: The Evaluation Panel may arrange for the debriefing of any unsuccessful Tenderers who request such a debriefing. The purpose of the debriefing is to provide Tenderers with general comments regarding evaluation of the Tendererâ€™s submission against the criteria. If undertaken, the debriefing process will be conducted by at least two members of the Evaluation Committee and may be carried out by telephone or letter. The debriefing process will be limited to the unsuccessful Tendererâ€™s offer. No comparisons will be made with the successful Tender and the debriefing process will not be used to justify the selection of the successful Tender. No scoring details will be provided.
tepp.json.contract_management: The contract will be managed by Manager City Fleet or delegate.
critical_dates: Commencement date in spec (01 Aug 2025) is earlier than policy minimum; adjusted to 11 Oct 2025 (â‰¥ title date + 26 days).
how_to_complete_tepp: 1) Title Page â€” Replace Tender Number, Name and Term.
2) 1. Aim â€” Update Tender Name and Term.
3) 2. Description of Requirement â€” Replace with contract description and main requirements.
4) 2.1 Desired Outcomes â€” Insert procurement outcomes (value for money, timely delivery, etc.).
5) 2.2 Purpose of RFT â€” Replace with reasons for this tender (or EOI/Quotation).
â€¦ (continue your internal guidance list as needed) â€¦
Update footer to correct Tender Number and Tender Name.

summary: The Blacktown Leisure Centre Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation AHU Refurbishments involve the installation of a new Reverse Cycle Water-Cooled Heat Pump Chiller, as part of an extensive refurbishment project. This includes updating and upgrading building management systems to integrate with the newly installed chillers.
The work is scheduled around the leisure centre's operating hours to minimize disruption during peak times.
Scope includes: Replacement/upgrade of water-cooled heat pump chillers; AHU coil refurbishments; 8 EC plug fans; BMCS integration (e.g., BACnet HLI)

tepp.json.shortlisting: Clearly non-competitive tenders may be excluded with reasons recorded.
alternative_tenders: Alternative tenders will be evaluated in accordance with the RFT evaluation methodology reproduced in this document.
tenderer_presentations: Tenderers may be invited to interview to clarify their tender. No new or additional information will be requested or permitted at this stage (see Section 3.8 â€“ Requests for Clarification). Answers must be factual and unbiased; no personal opinions are to be expressed.
evaluation_report: On completion of the evaluation, results will be documented in a Council Report including:
â€¢ a summary of the evaluation method,
â€¢ the number of tenders received,
â€¢ the relative ranking of tenders,
â€¢ the recommendation of the preferred tender, and
â€¢ the rationale used to select the preferred supplier.

The report will highlight key issues for negotiation/ongoing scrutiny, be considered by the Tender Review Committee, and, if approved, be endorsed by all Committee Members and presented to the Ordinary Council Meeting for adoption. The ultimate decision maker is Blacktown City Councilâ€™s elected officials.
tepp.json.debriefing_unsuccessful: Debriefing may be offered upon request. Only general comments on the tendererâ€™s own submission; no comparisons; no scoring details.

compliance_criteria: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS
Provide PPE for all on-site workers
Provide commissioning plan and evidence of tests per Section 10

required_criteria.name: Methodology
required_criteria.description: Commissioning plan covering: 10.1 General; Provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables, etc. to conduct testing and; commissioning. The Contractor shall carry out his own tests to verify operation and performance; before requesting Council to attend inspections. Submit fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection; prior to any testing taking place. The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and; precise record of all tests to be carried out.â€¦; BMCS integration/HLI (BACnet) with IO schedules and point lists; Program includes defects liability / warranty of 12 months; Provision for service/maintenance handover & schedules

required_criteria.name: Quality
required_criteria.description: Quality plan incl. ITPs; as-built docs; performance verification

required_criteria.name: Work Health & Safety
required_criteria.description: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS; Provide PPE for all on-site workers; Electrical work to AS/NZS 3000

interviews_presentations: Final scores may be revised following interviews/presentations conducted with shortlisted Tenderers, in accordance with Section 6.4 Tenderer Presentations.
shortlisting_alternatives: Where many tenders are received, clearly non-competitive tenders with no reasonable prospect of best value may be excluded from detailed evaluation. Reasons for exclusion must be defensible and recorded in the evaluation report.

metadata.project_title: BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments

Unless otherwise stated, all documents (schedules and supporting documents) must be submitted in PDF format.
Prices in ScheduleÂ 10 must be clear and unambiguous; replace zero/blank with 'included' or 'excluded' and provide an explanation if necessary. Council may reject tenders with ambiguous pricing.

sections.heading: Company Details
fields: Entity Type (e.g., Pty Ltd, Partnership, Sole Trader)

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have read and understood the Conditions of Tendering and agree to be bound by them, that we are solvent and not under any form of external administration, that we have disclosed any conflicts of interest, that we hold or will obtain the required insurances and licences, and that this tender constitutes a firm offer for the period specified.

sections.heading: Financial Statements
sections.content: Provide annual financial statements (balance sheet and profit/loss) for the last three years. If statements cannot be provided, explain why and supply alternative evidence of financial capacity.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.content: Acknowledge that all information provided by Council is confidential and must not be disclosed except as permitted.

sections.heading: Conflict of Interest
sections.content: Disclose any actual, potential or perceived conflicts of interest that may influence your ability to perform the contract. Include details of the conflict and the proposed mitigation strategy.

sections.heading: ICAC Guidance
sections.content: Refer to NSW ICAC guidance on managing conflicts of interest in procurement for further information.

sections.heading: Supporting Documents
sections.content: Attach curriculum vitae and/or licences evidencing qualifications and experience for each proposed key personnel.

rows: Professional Indemnity (min $10m â€“ maintained 7 years postâ€‘expiry)
Workersâ€™ Compensation (as required under NSW law)

sections.heading: Subcontractor Insurances
sections.content: List insurances held by all subcontractors. Tenderers may be required to increase cover limits if Council deems necessary.

sections.heading: Relevant Projects
sections.content: Provide details of at least three relevant projects completed in the last five years, including client name, project description, contract value, start and finish dates, and your scope of work.

sections.heading: Councils Serviced
sections.content: List any councils or local government organisations you are currently providing services to and the number of years you have been engaged.

sections.heading: Client Referees
sections.content: Provide details for at least two senior client referees (up to four may be provided).

sections.heading: Methodology & Resources
sections.content: Describe your methodology for delivering the works, including sequencing of activities, resource allocations (personnel, plant, equipment, suppliers and subcontractors), and how you will ensure quality outcomes.

sections.heading: Quality & Accreditations
sections.content: Outline your quality management system, including reference to any thirdâ€‘party accreditations and inspection test plans (ITPs). Attach sample reports where available.

sections.heading: Milestones

rows: Delivery and installation of major equipment
Handover and defects liability period commencement

sections.content: Populate the milestones table with the key deliverables for this project. If Council has mandated a fixed date for commencement or completion (e.g., start of operations by TBA), ensure your program aligns with that date.

sections.heading: Disruption Minimisation
sections.content: Explain how you will minimise disruption to Council operations and stakeholders during the works.

sections.heading: Q&A Confirmations
sections.content: Confirm that you will provide the following information as part of your tender or prior to award: product catalogues/technical sheets, draft layout diagrams/drawings, crew qualifications and experience, warranty terms, equipment origin (country of manufacture), and details of any proposed subcontractors.

sections.heading: Performance / Technical Specification
sections.content: List the key performance requirements and technical specifications for the major equipment or services to be supplied. For example, device capacities, ratings, compliance standards, control interfaces, and any basisâ€‘ofâ€‘design references.

sections.heading: Pricing Table
sections.content: Provide a lump sum price in Australian dollars for each scope package. Pricing must include GST, levies, duties, overheads and all other costs necessary to perform the works. Prices must be valid for the tender validity period.

sections.heading: CSR Policy / Initiatives
sections.content: Describe your organisationâ€™s corporate social responsibility policy or a relevant CSR initiative.

sections.heading: Sustainability Practices
sections.content: Explain your organisationâ€™s understanding of sustainability and outline the practices or initiatives you have in place to support sustainable procurement and operations.

sections.heading: Policies & Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your organisationâ€™s policies and evidence of practices relating to equal employment opportunity and fair employment. This may include recruitment and training policies, antiâ€‘discrimination and harassment policies, consultation and dispute resolution processes.

sections.heading: Industrial Relations & Commitments
sections.content: State any past breaches of wage or superannuation obligations and confirm rectification. Provide your commitments to employing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, apprentices and trainees, and your acknowledgement of the Industrial Relations Undertaking.

sections.heading: Acknowledgement of Council WHS Policy
sections.content: Acknowledge that you have read Councilâ€™s WHS Policy and will comply with all WHS obligations applicable to the contract.

sections.heading: Required WHS Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your WHS manuals, policies and procedures; descriptions of WHS responsibilities and consultation processes; sample checklists and inspection forms; records of WHS incidents and corrective actions; training records; risk assessments/SWMS; registers and SDS; plant and equipment maintenance records; permitâ€‘toâ€‘work samples; and a siteâ€‘specific WHS plan for a similar project.

sections.heading: Chain of Responsibility (CoR)
sections.content: If transport of goods is involved, provide evidence of your Chain of Responsibility compliance, including a CoR policy, risk register, KPIs, training records, fleet maintenance and diary records, and subcontractor preâ€‘engagement audits.

sections.heading: Required WHS Forms
sections.content: Attach the completed WHS Forms (AppendicesÂ 1â€“3) and WHS Policy (AppendixÂ 4).

sections.heading: Supplier Declarations
sections.content: Provide details of whether your organisation is reporting under the Australian Modern Slavery Act. Attach any modern slavery policies and describe your due diligence processes, training initiatives and supply chain risk assessments. List the countries of manufacture for products or components relevant to this tender.

sections.heading: Addenda Acknowledgement
sections.content: List all addenda issued for this RFT and indicate acknowledgment.

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have received all addenda listed above, that our tender complies with all requirements of the tender documents except as noted, that the information provided in all returnable schedules is true and correct, and that our offer remains open for the tender validity period (e.g., 120 days or as specified).

aim.intro: The Request for Tender (RFT) is for the Stanhope Gardens AHU to Council for the service of procurement, installation, and integration of specified works within a period as detailed in this context.
aim.body:
This Tender Evaluation and Probity Plan (TEPP) outlines: 
â€¢ The processes and principles that will guide the evaluation of tenders received. 
â€¢ Individual responsibilities to ensure compliance with standards, safety regulations, and timely completion of work.
â€¢ A detailed schedule for when various areas need to be accessible by contractors during installation works while minimizing disruption to leisner centre activities.
â€¢ Reporting requirements that include documentation on the progress, quality control measures taken, any deviations from plans or schedules with justifications and resolutions.

description_of_requirement: The works described in this specification involve refurbishing Stanhope Gardens AHU Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation, specifically for the Blacktown Leisure Centre (BLCS). The project includes installing a new reverse cycle water-cooled heat pump chiller and associated equipment to replace existing systems.

The scope of works extends beyond just mechanical services. It involves upgrading the Building Management Control System with additional control points, integrating BACnet High Level Interface gateways for seamless operation within the new system.

General:
â€¢ Licences: All work must be carried out by suitably qualified and certified/licensed tradespersons.
â€¢ Compliance with Australian Standards & NSW WHS regulations is mandatory for all aspects of installation, including WorkSafe NSW requirements.
â€¢ Schedule & Milestones: The Contractor shall submit a detailed schedule showing the proposed sequence and durations required to complete various areas without disrupting leis0ur centre's activities. This must be based on initial submission with Tender or as soon after award of tender as possible, considering Council's opening hours.
â€¢ Minimal Disruption: All noisy works shall consider the neighbouring properties and limited during typical working hours unless agreed upon by management to avoid disrupting leisure centre's activities. Interruptions for existing services are planned with minimal impact on operations.
â€¢ Comply with relevant AS/NZS standards and NSW WHS legislation
â€¢ Hold all required licences and insurances before commencing works
â€¢ Provide detailed program and milestones; notify of delays immediately
â€¢ Attend mandatory site visit and verify dimensions/conditions
technical_requirements:
Decommissioning of Existing Equipment:
â€¢ Ensure all materials removed from the site, which can be recycled (e.g., steel, copper, pipework, wiring), are transported to a designated recycling depot.
â€¢ Provide evidence of compliance with recycling requirements as per Council's environmental policies and legislation.
â€¢ Dispose of non-recyclable plastic components in accordance with statutory waste disposal regulations, ensuring no illegal dumping occurs on site or elsewhere.
â€¢ Remove existing filter bags from AHU Plenums & Associated Works as per the Cranage Strategy and ensure all works are carried out fully compliant with BCC WHS requirements.
â€¢ Install new 600x600, 600mm deep bag air filters in place of removed filter bags within AHU Plenums & Associated Works following the Cranage Strategy and Council's approval for hoisting equipment.
â€¢ Sand rusty areas, apply protective coatings as necessary to prepare surfaces before installing new aluminium checkered plates over existing discharge floors in plantrooms.
â€¢ Remove section of sheet metal ductwork above AHUs temporarily and install the larger redundant items into a separate area for delivery while ensuring minimal disturbance during their removal from other areas within the site.
â€¢ Coordinate with BVM/BLCS management to ensure safe access routes are maintained throughout all works, including equipment deliveries and retrievals of redundant items.

Switchboard Upgrade for Electrical Controls:
â€¢ Provide a plinth with SS Tray under the feet of each closed system.
â€¢ Install corrosion coupons in each closed system and test every three months as per AS/NZS standards, SafeWork NSW legislation, relevant council guidelines or any other S.A.A publications covering design, manufacture or installation of electrical equipment applicable to the local Electricity Supply Authority.
â€¢ Ensure all switchboards comply with AS 61439 and have a type test certificate from an accredited testing laboratory for performance substantiation as per S.C.A requirements.
â€¢ Install factory made, assembled free-standing floor mounted front connected enclosures without onsite assembly of the switchboard components to conform with AS 61439 standards and ensure all wiring is done according to Electrical Design Guide and AS/NZS standard AS3000.
â€¢ Arrange for neatly tied, supported cables where necessary in mechanical services plant room and building areas following the requirements of the Electrical Design Guide and NSW Standards (AS 3000).
â€¢ Install cable trays with supports as per Australian Standard AS2470.1-2006 for all conduits, ducts, cables in mechanical services plant room.
â€¢ Extend the existing Building Management Control System to control new works and reprogram BMCS logic identically to newly refurbished AHU 2 as per council best practices while ensuring no waver of responsibility between HVAC and BMCS contractors is allowed, with coordination for inter-related works.
â€¢ Install necessary additional points in the Building Management Control System (BMS) to control new equipment including heat recovery circulation pumps and EC fans as per council best practices while enssuring compatibility with existing systems. Integrate a Chiller BACnet High Level Interface gateway into the BMCS for complete system operation.
â€¢ Supply, install adequately sized power cables from chiller sub-board to main switchboard in compliance with NSW/Australian standards and council best practices ensuring correct control and status on Control as per drawings. Replace electrical components within the board if necessary for safety purposes.
â€¢ Extend existing BMCS logic up to end of Defects Liability Period without additional cost, allowing progressive fine-tuning after commissioning in compliance with council best practices while ensuring all wiring penetrating a fire wall is installed using specialized intumescent fittings as per NSW/Australian standards.
â€¢ Remove louvres temporarily to allow delivery of new items into the plantroom including chillers, coils etc. in one operation with BVM/BLCS management's agreement and ensure all disrupted areas are made good post-installation following council best practices.

Installation of New Chiller System and Associated Pipework Modifications:
â€¢ Provide manufacturer's rated performance data for the chiller.
â€¢ Supply full dimensional details in certified drawings, including weights with loading diagrams.
â€¢ Detail facilities and services required by the Chiller offered.
â€¢ List accessories and features provided with the Chiller offered.
â€¢ Specify electrical requirements for the Chiller.
â€¢ Include wiring diagrams as part of product data provision.
â€¢ Evidence conformance to AS1677.2 and AS1210 standards, along with SafeWork NSW legislation compliance.
â€¢ Engage local manufacturer's distributor for maintenance during Defects Liability Period in Australia without additional cost.
â€¢ Clear the area where new chiller will be positioned by forming 90mm high concrete plinths, allowing time for cure before delivery of the chiller.
â€¢ Extend existing Building Management Control System to control and monitor all works related to heating/ventilation systems including AHU refurbishments. Re-engineer BMCS logic as per new requirements with a focus on maintaining current Chiller Staging strategy, complete programming for integration of the new chiller's High Level Interface gateway.
â€¢ Supply and install condensate sumps common to cool side heat recovery coils including evaporator coils. Install drift eliminators as required by NSW standards.
â€¢ Install all associated pipework with necessary valves, fittings for chilled water system interconnecting works such as bleeds, drains and isolation items like gages cocks in accordance to BCC WHS requirements.
â€¢ Insulate condenser water pipework using foil faced foam shield type insulation. Sheath at low level under 1.8m with minimum vibration isolation efficiency of 95% as per NSW standards.
â€¢ Pressure test and evacuate all pipe works, then fully factory charge the system with refrigerant before delivery to Site for operation readiness.
â€¢ Provide three sets of drawings including Workshop Drawings; Plan, Sections and Details (1:50 scale); Electrical Single Line Diagram; Automatic Controls Schematic Diagrams along with 'As-Installed' Drawings in Autocad and PDF USB 3 format.
â€¢ Ensure all works are carried out fully compliant to proper BCC WHS requirements, including the use of hoisting facilities for equipment installation as per council approvals. All disturbed work shall be made good with full satisfaction from Council.

Extension of Building Management Control System (BMCS) to Include Refurbished AHU 2 Logic:
â€¢ Extend the existing BMCS and re-engineer and reprogram for complete control over new ventilation and heating systems.
â€¢ Integrate a Chiller BACnet High Level Interface gateway with the Building Management System (BMS) to operate as specified, providing Device Instance details and carrying out necessary programming post Commissioning up to end of Defects Liability Period without additional cost.
â€¢ Test and commission new installation ensuring it aligns with refurbished AHU 2 logic for consistent performance. Allow progressive fine-tuning of control logic after testing until the end of the Defects Liability Period, at no extra charge to Contract.
â€¢ Comply with relevant Australian Standards and Council WHS requirements throughout all works including water treatment following Water Treatment Design Guide requirements for each closed system. Use SS Tray under feet drained to waste as per standard practice.
â€¢ All work shall be carried out by suitably qualified, licensed tradespersons experienced in relevant trade with compliance to WorkSafe NSW regulations and Council WHS standards.
â€¢ Drawings provided for information purposes include M114 - Mechanical Services â€“ Air & Water Schematic (Existing Existing Equipment), SKM-01 AHU 1&3 Piping Schematic Drawing, among others as per standard practice and council requirements.

tender_evaluation.methodology_text: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced here, will evaluate all tenders.

required_criteria_table.rationale: Given the high commissioning complexity and safety critical nature of this project with a warranty period extending up to one year, it's crucial that cost does not exceed our budget constraints. Therefore, we allocate more weight here

With 8 EC fans in operation requiring specialized knowledge for integration into the existing system and considering past performance on similar projects is essential; hence a significant portion of the evaluation will be based on experience to ensure reliability and efficiency

The capability score reflects our need for robust, reliable equipment that can handle complex tasks with minimal downtime. A higher weight ensures we select suppliers who are not only experienced but also technically capable of delivering the required performance standards

Adegya's financial stability and management skills will be crucial for handling potential cost overruns or unforeseen expenses, hence a moderate weight is assigned to this criterion. It ensures that we can rely on their ability to manage the project effectively

Given our commitment to maintaining high-quality standards and minimizing disru0125 7e9rsees, quality management holds a significant weight in selection as it directly impacts system reliability. A higher score here indicates better adherence to industry best practices
Workplace Health & Safety is important but given the lower risk factors associated with this project and its non-critical nature compared to others, we assign a slightly lesser weight

evaluation_methodology.overview: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the evaluation methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced in this document, will evaluate all tenders. Compliance is generally taken to mean submission of the Tender by the closing date, in accordance with all lodgement instructions and provision of all information requested in the RFT (including any late tender provisions). Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted; depending on the nature, extent and implications of partial or non-compliance, a Tender may be disqualified from further evaluation.

compliance_criteria_table.title: 6.1.1 Compliance Criteria
notes: Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted. Non-compliant tenders may be excluded from further evaluation.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Exceptional
non_price_rating_scale.description: Fully compliant; no risks or weaknesses.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Superior
non_price_rating_scale.description: Sound achievement; minor risks/weaknesses may be acceptable.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Good
non_price_rating_scale.description: Reasonable achievement; errors/risks can be corrected with minimal effort.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Adequate
non_price_rating_scale.description: Minimal achievement; issues possible to correct/overcome.

returnable_scoring.note: See evaluation workbook and schedules for detailed rubrics.

pricing.pricing_model_text:
Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score (equal to the price weighting). State Government method:
Ps = 200 â€“ (Pc / Pav Ã— 100)
Pn = Ps / 200
Pw = Pn Ã— [Price Weight]
Pc = Total Net Cost, Pav = Average of Total Net Costs.

local_preference_policy.blacktown_lga_discount: 5% discount capped at $50,000 for suppliers located in Blacktown City LGA
local_preference_policy.western_sydney_discount: 2.5% discount capped at $25,000 for suppliers located in Western Sydney councils
definitions: Blacktown City Local supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within Blacktown City LGA.
Western Sydney Council supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within a Western Sydney Council LGA.
local_preference_policy.application_note: For any suppliers eligible for local preference, provide the confirming address in the third tab of the tender evaluation spreadsheet.

value_for_money: Assessed on qualitative outcomes + price: quality and fitness for purpose vs whole-of-life costs vs risk.
Consider whole-of-life costs incl. maintenance and warranty obligations
Energy efficiency and control strategy for chillers/AHUs
â€˜Value for Moneyâ€™ is assessed on the combined outcomes of qualitative criteria and price.
Consider quality of proposed services against whole-of-life costs and risk (capacity to deliver on-time and on-budget).
pricing.interviews_and_presentation_criteria: Scores may be revised following interviews/presentations of shortlisted tenderers.

model.narrative: Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score equal to the Price criterion weighting. In accordance with State Government scoring, the Total Net Cost for tender evaluation is normalised and weighted for comparison as follows.
variables: Pc = Total Net Cost calculated for tender evaluation
Pav = Average of all Total Net Costs
notes: The total score each Tenderer receives provides a numeric basis for comparison.
Recommendation on the preferred Tender will be based on scoring comparisons.
The basis of any increase will be calculated for the full term of the contract, including optional extensions.

tepp.json.contract_negotiations: A period of negotiation with the successful Tenderer may arise following completion of the Tender phase and will commence subject to approval by the Director. This period will result in the executed copies of the contractual documentation being completed and will necessitate a combination of meetings comprising formal written obligations to be resolved. The role of the negotiators is to ensure that the negotiation approach is appropriate to the nature of the project, is open and fair, meets the needs of the Tenderer and can be accommodated within the Tendererâ€™s resources. Negotiations must not result in material changes to the requirements of the Tender. The outcome of the negotiations will be reflected in the final contract and all negotiation discussions and outcomes will be documented.
tepp.json.debriefing: The Evaluation Panel may arrange for the debriefing of any unsuccessful Tenderers who request such a debriefing. The purpose of the debriefing is to provide Tenderers with general comments regarding evaluation of the Tendererâ€™s submission against the criteria. If undertaken, the debriefing process will be conducted by at least two members of the Evaluation Committee and may be carried out by telephone or letter. The debriefing process will be limited to the unsuccessful Tendererâ€™s offer. No comparisons will be made with the successful Tender and the debriefing process will not be used to justify the selection of the successful Tender. No scoring details will be provided.
tepp.json.contract_management: The contract will be managed by Manager City Fleet or delegate.
critical_dates: Commencement date in spec (01 Aug 2025) is earlier than policy minimum; adjusted to 11 Oct 2025 (â‰¥ title date + 26 days).
how_to_complete_tepp: 1) Title Page â€” Replace Tender Number, Name and Term.
2) 1. Aim â€” Update Tender Name and Term.
3) 2. Description of Requirement â€” Replace with contract description and main requirements.
4) 2.1 Desired Outcomes â€” Insert procurement outcomes (value for money, timely delivery, etc.).
5) 2.2 Purpose of RFT â€” Replace with reasons for this tender (or EOI/Quotation).
â€¦ (continue your internal guidance list as needed) â€¦
Update footer to correct Tender Number and Tender Name.

summary: The works described in this specification involve refurbishing Stanhope Gardens AHU Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation, specifically for the Blacktown Leisure Centre (BLCS). The project includes installing a new reverse cycle water-cooled heat pump chiller and associated equipment to replace existing systems.

The scope of works extends beyond just mechanical services. It involves upgrading the Building Management Control System with additional control points, integrating BACnet High Level Interface gateways for seamless operation within the new system.
Scope includes: Replacement/upgrade of water-cooled heat pump chillers; AHU coil refurbishments; 8 EC plug fans; BMCS integration (e.g., BACnet HLI)

tepp.json.shortlisting: Clearly non-competitive tenders may be excluded with reasons recorded.
alternative_tenders: Alternative tenders will be evaluated in accordance with the RFT evaluation methodology reproduced in this document.
tenderer_presentations: Tenderers may be invited to interview to clarify their tender. No new or additional information will be requested or permitted at this stage (see Section 3.8 â€“ Requests for Clarification). Answers must be factual and unbiased; no personal opinions are to be expressed.
evaluation_report: On completion of the evaluation, results will be documented in a Council Report including:
â€¢ a summary of the evaluation method,
â€¢ the number of tenders received,
â€¢ the relative ranking of tenders,
â€¢ the recommendation of the preferred tender, and
â€¢ the rationale used to select the preferred supplier.

The report will highlight key issues for negotiation/ongoing scrutiny, be considered by the Tender Review Committee, and, if approved, be endorsed by all Committee Members and presented to the Ordinary Council Meeting for adoption. The ultimate decision maker is Blacktown City Councilâ€™s elected officials.
tepp.json.debriefing_unsuccessful: Debriefing may be offered upon request. Only general comments on the tendererâ€™s own submission; no comparisons; no scoring details.

The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and; precise record of all tests to be carried out.â€¦; BMCS integration/HLI (BACnet) with IO schedules and point lists; Program includes defects liability / warranty of 12 months; Provision for service/maintenance handover & schedules

shortlisting_alternatives: Where many tenders are received, clearly non-competitive tenders with no reasonable prospect of best value may be excluded from detailed evaluation. Reasons for exclusion must be defensible and recorded in the evaluation report.

sections.heading: Company Details
fields: Entity Type (e.g., Pty Ltd, Partnership, Sole Trader)

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have read and understood the Conditions of Tendering and agree to be bound by them, that we are solvent and not under any form of external administration, that we have disclosed any conflicts of interest, that we hold or will obtain the required insurances and licences, and that this tender constitutes a firm offer for the period specified.

sections.heading: Financial Statements
sections.content: Provide annual financial statements (balance sheet and profit/loss) for the last three years. If statements cannot be provided, explain why and supply alternative evidence of financial capacity.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.content: Acknowledge that all information provided by Council is confidential and must not be disclosed except as permitted.

sections.heading: Conflict of Interest
sections.content: Disclose any actual, potential or perceived conflicts of interest that may influence your ability to perform the contract. Include details of the conflict and the proposed mitigation strategy.

sections.heading: ICAC Guidance
sections.content: Refer to NSW ICAC guidance on managing conflicts of interest in procurement for further information.

sections.heading: Supporting Documents
sections.content: Attach curriculum vitae and/or licences evidencing qualifications and experience for each proposed key personnel.

rows: Professional Indemnity (min $10m â€“ maintained 7 years postâ€‘expiry)
Workersâ€™ Compensation (as required under NSW law)

sections.heading: Subcontractor Insurances
sections.content: List insurances held by all subcontractors. Tenderers may be required to increase cover limits if Council deems necessary.

sections.heading: Relevant Projects
sections.content: Provide details of at least three relevant projects completed in the last five years, including client name, project description, contract value, start and finish dates, and your scope of work.

sections.heading: Councils Serviced
sections.content: List any councils or local government organisations you are currently providing services to and the number of years you have been engaged.

sections.heading: Client Referees
sections.content: Provide details for at least two senior client referees (up to four may be provided).

sections.heading: Methodology & Resources
sections.content: Describe your methodology for delivering the works, including sequencing of activities, resource allocations (personnel, plant, equipment, suppliers and subcontractors), and how you will ensure quality outcomes.

sections.heading: Quality & Accreditations
sections.content: Outline your quality management system, including reference to any thirdâ€‘party accreditations and inspection test plans (ITPs). Attach sample reports where available.

sections.heading: Milestones

rows: Delivery and installation of major equipment
Handover and defects liability period commencement

sections.content: Populate the milestones table with the key deliverables for this project. If Council has mandated a fixed date for commencement or completion (e.g., start of operations by TBA), ensure your program aligns with that date.

sections.heading: Disruption Minimisation
sections.content: Explain how you will minimise disruption to Council operations and stakeholders during the works.

sections.heading: Q&A Confirmations
sections.content: Confirm that you will provide the following information as part of your tender or prior to award: product catalogues/technical sheets, draft layout diagrams/drawings, crew qualifications and experience, warranty terms, equipment origin (country of manufacture), and details of any proposed subcontractors.

sections.heading: Performance / Technical Specification
sections.content: List the key performance requirements and technical specifications for the major equipment or services to be supplied. For example, device capacities, ratings, compliance standards, control interfaces, and any basisâ€‘ofâ€‘design references.

sections.heading: Pricing Table
sections.content: Provide a lump sum price in Australian dollars for each scope package. Pricing must include GST, levies, duties, overheads and all other costs necessary to perform the works. Prices must be valid for the tender validity period.

sections.heading: CSR Policy / Initiatives
sections.content: Describe your organisationâ€™s corporate social responsibility policy or a relevant CSR initiative.

sections.heading: Sustainability Practices
sections.content: Explain your organisationâ€™s understanding of sustainability and outline the practices or initiatives you have in place to support sustainable procurement and operations.

sections.heading: Policies & Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your organisationâ€™s policies and evidence of practices relating to equal employment opportunity and fair employment. This may include recruitment and training policies, antiâ€‘discrimination and harassment policies, consultation and dispute resolution processes.

sections.heading: Industrial Relations & Commitments
sections.content: State any past breaches of wage or superannuation obligations and confirm rectification. Provide your commitments to employing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, apprentices and trainees, and your acknowledgement of the Industrial Relations Undertaking.

sections.heading: Acknowledgement of Council WHS Policy
sections.content: Acknowledge that you have read Councilâ€™s WHS Policy and will comply with all WHS obligations applicable to the contract.

sections.heading: Required WHS Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your WHS manuals, policies and procedures; descriptions of WHS responsibilities and consultation processes; sample checklists and inspection forms; records of WHS incidents and corrective actions; training records; risk assessments/SWMS; registers and SDS; plant and equipment maintenance records; permitâ€‘toâ€‘work samples; and a siteâ€‘specific WHS plan for a similar project.

sections.heading: Chain of Responsibility (CoR)
sections.content: If transport of goods is involved, provide evidence of your Chain of Responsibility compliance, including a CoR policy, risk register, KPIs, training records, fleet maintenance and diary records, and subcontractor preâ€‘engagement audits.

sections.heading: Required WHS Forms
sections.content: Attach the completed WHS Forms (AppendicesÂ 1â€“3) and WHS Policy (AppendixÂ 4).

sections.heading: Supplier Declarations
sections.content: Provide details of whether your organisation is reporting under the Australian Modern Slavery Act. Attach any modern slavery policies and describe your due diligence processes, training initiatives and supply chain risk assessments. List the countries of manufacture for products or components relevant to this tender.

sections.heading: Addenda Acknowledgement
sections.content: List all addenda issued for this RFT and indicate acknowledgment.

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have received all addenda listed above, that our tender complies with all requirements of the tender documents except as noted, that the information provided in all returnable schedules is true and correct, and that our offer remains open for the tender validity period (e.g., 120 days or as specified).

aim.intro: The Request for Tender (RFT) is for the Fleet Vehicle Replacement Program to Council for the service of the procurement, installation, and integration of the specified works. For a term of 12 months.

description_of_requirement: This procurement relates to Fleet Vehicle Replacement Program for Council.

General:
• Comply with relevant AS/NZS standards and NSW WHS legislation
• Hold all required licences and insurances before commencing works
• Provide detailed program and milestones; notify of delays immediately
• Attend mandatory site visit and verify dimensions/conditions
• Minimise disruption to Council operations and the public

Decommissioning & Removal:
• Provide method statements and SWMS for all high-risk activities
• Coordinate works with other trades and Council representatives
• Verify dimensions and services prior to fabrication/installation
• Test and commission to manufacturer and AS/NZS requirements
• Provide as-built documentation and O&M manuals at completion

Delivery & Logistics:
• Provide method statements and SWMS for all high-risk activities
• Coordinate works with other trades and Council representatives
• Verify dimensions and services prior to fabrication/installation
• Test and commission to manufacturer and AS/NZS requirements
• Provide as-built documentation and O&M manuals at completion

Supply & Installation:
• Provide method statements and SWMS for all high-risk activities
• Coordinate works with other trades and Council representatives
• Verify dimensions and services prior to fabrication/installation
• Test and commission to manufacturer and AS/NZS requirements
• Provide as-built documentation and O&M manuals at completion

Testing & Commissioning:
• Provide method statements and SWMS for all high-risk activities
• Coordinate works with other trades and Council representatives
• Verify dimensions and services prior to fabrication/installation
• Test and commission to manufacturer and AS/NZS requirements
• Provide as-built documentation and O&M manuals at completion

Maintenance & Warranty:
• Provide method statements and SWMS for all high-risk activities
• Coordinate works with other trades and Council representatives
• Verify dimensions and services prior to fabrication/installation
• Test and commission to manufacturer and AS/NZS requirements
• Provide as-built documentation and O&M manuals at completion

Proven experience reduces delivery risk. Final weight used: 14.3%.
Financial stability & governance mitigate schedule and performance risk. Final weight used: 9.5%.
Financial stability & governance mitigate schedule and performance risk. Quality controls minimise rework and defects. Final weight used: 7.2%.
Safety-critical activities demand strong WHS (risk level 1). Final weight used: 7.2%.

Negotiations must not result in material changes to the requirements of the Tender. The outcome of the negotiations will be reflected in the final contract and all negotiation discussions and outcomes will be documented.
tepp.json.debriefing: The Evaluation Panel may arrange for the debriefing of any unsuccessful Tenderers who request such a debriefing. The purpose of the debriefing is to provide Tenderers with general comments regarding evaluation of the Tenderer’s submission against the criteria. If undertaken, the debriefing process will be conducted by at least two members of the Evaluation Committee and may be carried out by telephone or letter. The debriefing process will be limited to the unsuccessful Tenderer’s offer. No comparisons will be made with the successful Tender and the debriefing process will not be used to justify the selection of the successful Tender. No scoring details will be provided.
tepp.json.contract_management: The contract will be managed by Manager City Fleet or delegate.
critical_dates: No explicit commencement date found in spec; set to policy minimum 17 Oct 2025 (≥ title date + 26 days).
how_to_complete_tepp: 1) Title Page — Replace Tender Number, Name and Term.
2) 1. Aim — Update Tender Name and Term.
3) 2. Description of Requirement — Replace with contract description and main requirements.
4) 2.1 Desired Outcomes — Insert procurement outcomes (value for money, timely delivery, etc.).
5) 2.2 Purpose of RFT — Replace with reasons for this tender (or EOI/Quotation).
… (continue your internal guidance list as needed) …
Update footer to correct Tender Number and Tender Name.

summary: This procurement relates to Fleet Vehicle Replacement Program for Council.

tepp.json.shortlisting: Clearly non-competitive tenders may be excluded with reasons recorded.
alternative_tenders: Alternative tenders will be evaluated in accordance with the RFT evaluation methodology reproduced in this document.
tenderer_presentations: Tenderers may be invited to interview to clarify their tender. No new or additional information will be requested or permitted at this stage (see Section 3.8 – Requests for Clarification). Answers must be factual and unbiased; no personal opinions are to be expressed.
evaluation_report: On completion of the evaluation, results will be documented in a Council Report including:
• a summary of the evaluation method,
• the number of tenders received,
• the relative ranking of tenders,
• the recommendation of the preferred tender, and
• the rationale used to select the preferred supplier.

The report will highlight key issues for negotiation/ongoing scrutiny, be considered by the Tender Review Committee, and, if approved, be endorsed by all Committee Members and presented to the Ordinary Council Meeting for adoption. The ultimate decision maker is Blacktown City Council’s elected officials.
tepp.json.debriefing_unsuccessful: Debriefing may be offered upon request. Only general comments on the tenderer’s own submission; no comparisons; no scoring details.

compliance_criteria: Provide PPE for all on-site workers

required_criteria.name: Quality
required_criteria.description: Quality plan incl. ITPs; as-built docs; performance verification

required_criteria.name: Work Health & Safety
required_criteria.description: Provide PPE for all on-site workers

interviews_presentations: Final scores may be revised following interviews/presentations conducted with shortlisted Tenderers, in accordance with Section 6.4 Tenderer Presentations.
shortlisting_alternatives: Where many tenders are received, clearly non-competitive tenders with no reasonable prospect of best value may be excluded from detailed evaluation. Reasons for exclusion must be defensible and recorded in the evaluation report.

instructions: Use the provided templates to complete the required Schedules.
Provide the requested information using the DOCX file and submit as a PDF.
If a schedule does not have enough space, include the information in a separate attachment and reference it in the schedule.
Unless otherwise stated, all documents (schedules and supporting documents) must be submitted in PDF format.
Prices in Schedule 10 must be clear and unambiguous; replace zero/blank with 'included' or 'excluded' and provide an explanation if necessary. Council may reject tenders with ambiguous pricing.
supporting_information: Compliance with the tender requirements
Relevant experience, qualifications and licences
Capacity, including past performance and resources
Methodology to undertake the works
Management & financial capacity (including financial statements for the last 3 years)
Quality management (policy, management plan, forms and checklists)
WHS management (completed Council WHS forms, WHS policy & plan, safe work method statements, checklists and forms)
Sustainable environmental management (policy and plan)
Copies of certificates for required third‑party accreditations and insurances
Equal employment opportunity and fair employment evidence
Any additional relevant information that may assist in assessing the tender

supporting_documents: Quality management plans and inspection test plans
WHS manuals, policies, sample checklists and forms

sections.heading: Company Details
fields: Entity Type (e.g., Pty Ltd, Partnership, Sole Trader)

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have read and understood the Conditions of Tendering and agree to be bound by them, that we are solvent and not under any form of external administration, that we have disclosed any conflicts of interest, that we hold or will obtain the required insurances and licences, and that this tender constitutes a firm offer for the period specified.

sections.heading: Financial Statements
sections.content: Provide annual financial statements (balance sheet and profit/loss) for the last three years. If statements cannot be provided, explain why and supply alternative evidence of financial capacity.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.content: Acknowledge that all information provided by Council is confidential and must not be disclosed except as permitted.

sections.heading: Conflict of Interest
sections.content: Disclose any actual, potential or perceived conflicts of interest that may influence your ability to perform the contract. Include details of the conflict and the proposed mitigation strategy.

sections.heading: ICAC Guidance
sections.content: Refer to NSW ICAC guidance on managing conflicts of interest in procurement for further information.

sections.heading: Supporting Documents
sections.content: Attach curriculum vitae and/or licences evidencing qualifications and experience for each proposed key personnel.

rows: Workers’ Compensation (as required under NSW law)

sections.heading: Subcontractor Insurances
sections.content: List insurances held by all subcontractors. Tenderers may be required to increase cover limits if Council deems necessary.

sections.heading: Relevant Projects
sections.content: Provide details of at least three relevant projects completed in the last five years, including client name, project description, contract value, start and finish dates, and your scope of work.

sections.heading: Councils Serviced
sections.content: List any councils or local government organisations you are currently providing services to and the number of years you have been engaged.

sections.heading: Client Referees
sections.content: Provide details for at least two senior client referees (up to four may be provided).

sections.heading: Methodology & Resources
sections.content: Describe your methodology for delivering the works, including sequencing of activities, resource allocations (personnel, plant, equipment, suppliers and subcontractors), and how you will ensure quality outcomes.

sections.heading: Quality & Accreditations
sections.content: Outline your quality management system, including reference to any third‑party accreditations and inspection test plans (ITPs). Attach sample reports where available.

sections.heading: Milestones

rows: Delivery and installation of major equipment
Handover and defects liability period commencement

sections.content: Populate the milestones table with the key deliverables for this project. If Council has mandated a fixed date for commencement or completion (e.g., start of operations by TBA), ensure your program aligns with that date.

sections.heading: Disruption Minimisation
sections.content: Explain how you will minimise disruption to Council operations and stakeholders during the works.

sections.heading: Q&A Confirmations
sections.content: Confirm that you will provide the following information as part of your tender or prior to award: product catalogues/technical sheets, draft layout diagrams/drawings, crew qualifications and experience, warranty terms, equipment origin (country of manufacture), and details of any proposed subcontractors.

sections.heading: Performance / Technical Specification
sections.content: List the key performance requirements and technical specifications for the major equipment or services to be supplied. For example, device capacities, ratings, compliance standards, control interfaces, and any basis‑of‑design references.

sections.heading: Pricing Table
sections.content: Provide a lump sum price in Australian dollars for each scope package. Pricing must include GST, levies, duties, overheads and all other costs necessary to perform the works. Prices must be valid for the tender validity period.

sections.heading: CSR Policy / Initiatives
sections.content: Describe your organisation’s corporate social responsibility policy or a relevant CSR initiative.

sections.heading: Sustainability Practices
sections.content: Explain your organisation’s understanding of sustainability and outline the practices or initiatives you have in place to support sustainable procurement and operations.

sections.heading: Policies & Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your organisation’s policies and evidence of practices relating to equal employment opportunity and fair employment. This may include recruitment and training policies, anti‑discrimination and harassment policies, consultation and dispute resolution processes.

sections.heading: Industrial Relations & Commitments
sections.content: State any past breaches of wage or superannuation obligations and confirm rectification. Provide your commitments to employing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, apprentices and trainees, and your acknowledgement of the Industrial Relations Undertaking.

sections.heading: Acknowledgement of Council WHS Policy
sections.content: Acknowledge that you have read Council’s WHS Policy and will comply with all WHS obligations applicable to the contract.

sections.heading: Required WHS Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your WHS manuals, policies and procedures; descriptions of WHS responsibilities and consultation processes; sample checklists and inspection forms; records of WHS incidents and corrective actions; training records; risk assessments/SWMS; registers and SDS; plant and equipment maintenance records; permit‑to‑work samples; and a site‑specific WHS plan for a similar project.

sections.heading: Chain of Responsibility (CoR)
sections.content: If transport of goods is involved, provide evidence of your Chain of Responsibility compliance, including a CoR policy, risk register, KPIs, training records, fleet maintenance and diary records, and subcontractor pre‑engagement audits.

sections.heading: Required WHS Forms
sections.content: Attach the completed WHS Forms (Appendices 1–3) and WHS Policy (Appendix 4).

sections.heading: Supplier Declarations
sections.content: Provide details of whether your organisation is reporting under the Australian Modern Slavery Act. Attach any modern slavery policies and describe your due diligence processes, training initiatives and supply chain risk assessments. List the countries of manufacture for products or components relevant to this tender.

sections.heading: Addenda Acknowledgement
sections.content: List all addenda issued for this RFT and indicate acknowledgment.

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have received all addenda listed above, that our tender complies with all requirements of the tender documents except as noted, that the information provided in all returnable schedules is true and correct, and that our offer remains open for the tender validity period (e.g., 120 days or as specified).
